summary: AWQ GEMV kernel (gemv_int4_v5_awq.hip) is correctly implemented, compiles for gfx906, and passes correctness checks (max_abs_err=0.000739 << 1e-2). The kernel removes the zeros pointer parameter entirely and saves 8 v_sub_f32 instructions per uint32 word. Engine integration with set_awq_mode() is well-structured and propagates to all TP engines. Performance improvement is real: 1.17-1.27x speedup on isolated GEMV vs GPTQ.

codeReviewIssues.description: AWQ mode only applies to non-residual GEMV calls (_launch_gemv_int4 with residual=0). The down_proj with residual epilogue falls through to the standard GPTQ kernel even in AWQ mode. This means the down_proj GEMV still uses the GPTQ kernel with zeros pointer.
codeReviewIssues.suggestion: Add AWQ variant for residual GEMV path too, or document that AWQ optimization only covers non-residual calls (gate, up projections). Non-blocking as zeros=0 means GPTQ kernel gives correct results.

codeReviewIssues.description: The dual fused kernel (_launch_ffn_gate_up_silu / gemv_int4_dual) is used when self._gemv_int4_dual is True, which bypasses AWQ mode entirely (the dual kernel still passes zeros ptr). In practice, AWQ mode is set on the standard single-projection path only.
codeReviewIssues.suggestion: Worker handoff already documents this as a known limitation. Non-blocking.

codeReviewIssues.description: AWQ mode bypassed by C dispatch: The C dispatch plan caches kernel function pointers at build time and ignores set_awq_mode() called afterward. This means AWQ speedup is not realized in c_dispatch path. This pre-existing gap is documented in the E2E worker handoff.
codeReviewIssues.suggestion: Future work: rebuild c_dispatch plan after set_awq_mode(). Not a correctness issue.

codeReviewIssues.description: The kernel uses __builtin_amdgcn_ubfe() for nibble extraction (unsigned bit field extract) which is correct for AWQ's 0..15 unsigned range. GPTQ v5 also uses the same builtin. Consistent and correct.

validationAssertionsChecked.evidence: Worker handoff confirms: max_abs_err=0.000739 < 1e-2 for all 5 Qwen 3.5 27B shapes (N=4096/5120/11008/13696/17408, K=5120). Equivalence check: AWQ == GPTQ(zeros=0) exactly (error=0.0). Both pass. Scrutiny validator confirmed awq_dequantize correctness inline (max_err=0.000000 for small test case).

sharedStateObservations.suggestion: Document in architecture.md that AWQ kernel (gemv_int4_v5_awq) bypasses c_dispatch path — isolated speedup (1.17-1.27x) is not realized in E2E because c_dispatch caches GPTQ function pointers.
sharedStateObservations.rationale: Future workers should know about this gap when comparing AWQ E2E vs isolated benchmarks.
